hyperedges.label: Turn execution pipeline: ConversationAgent -> CapabilityBroker -> CapabilityExecutor
Context bundle assembly: MemoryBroker aggregates ContextStore + PersonaManager for each turn
Routing decision flow: CommandRouter -> RoutingState -> ResponseFinalizer
Per-turn execution pipeline: TurnManager orchestrates TurnContext, trace_scope, and memory in each turn
ResultCache, OrderedToolExecutor, and TaskGraphExecutor form the tool result caching and execution subsystem
TurnContext, RoutingState, and DialogState form the unified per-turn ephemeral state bundle
v2 Turn Planning Pipeline
Extension Adapter Pattern
Memory Triad (Episodic+Semantic+Procedural)
LangGraph execution pipeline: GraphCompiler + workflows + GraphState
Deterministic routing layer: RouteScorer + ModelRouter + CapabilityBroker
HUD visual state indicators: ParticleGlobeReactor + PulseBars + RadarPanel
Plugin Tool Registration Pattern via app.router
LLM Inference Lock Sharing across research, chat, and file summarization
File Operation Pipeline: search, read, summarize
Voice I/O pipeline: audio capture, transcription, safety gating, command dispatch
Reminder/calendar creation flow: parse datetime, insert event, schedule timer and notification
Double-clap startup pipeline: detect clap, check FRIDAY state, launch FRIDAY, autostart registration
Camera-based perception pipeline: capture, detect, stream
v2 planning layer: intent classification, plan building, workflow resumption
WhatsApp send pipeline: skill -> client -> driver
STT/TTS Voice Pipeline with Barge-In
Router Tool Dispatch and Multi-Action Execution Flow
v2 Turn Orchestration through RuntimeKernel
TurnOrchestrator pipeline: WorkflowCoordinator, IntentEngine, ToolPlan executor
Hybrid architecture app core: CapabilityRegistry, ContextStore, ConversationAgent, WorkflowOrchestrator
Workflow orchestration via plugins: TaskManagerPlugin, SystemControlPlugin, BrowserAutomationPlugin with CommandRouter

nodes.label: Local-model-first AI assistant design
nodes.rationale: FRIDAY keeps all reasoning local (llama-cpp-python with GGUF models) to avoid hosted dependency; online skills are opt-in with explicit user consent (ask_before_online).

nodes.label: MCP-compatible internal capability registry
nodes.rationale: CapabilityRegistry is shaped to MCP-style contracts so external MCP servers can be adopted later without rewriting the conversation layer.

nodes.label: Hybrid SQLite + Chroma memory architecture
nodes.rationale: SQLite handles structured identity/session/workflow data; ChromaDB provides semantic recall via HashEmbeddingFunction (no heavy embedding model needed). Falls back to keyword overlap when Chroma unavailable.

nodes.label: Strangler-fig migration pattern
nodes.rationale: FRIDAY 2.0 uses a strangler-fig migration pattern so the assistant remains fully functional at every phase boundary — no big-bang rewrite. The old CommandRouter is kept alive and load-bearing while each phase chips away at its responsibilities.

nodes.label: LangGraph optional execution engine feature flag
nodes.rationale: GraphCompiler uses a config flag routing.execution_engine to toggle between 'ordered' (OrderedToolExecutor fallback) and 'graph' (LangGraph StateGraph). If langgraph is not installed it silently falls back to the ordered executor — enabling optional dependency without breaking the system.
nodes.norm_label: langgraph optional execution engine feature flag

nodes.label: Three-tier memory architecture (Episodic/Semantic/Procedural)
nodes.rationale: Memory is split into Episodic (rolling 30-day turns), Semantic (key-value facts with confidence), and Procedural (bandit-style capability success rates) to match different lifecycle and access patterns.

nodes.label: RouteScorer extraction from CommandRouter rationale
nodes.rationale: RouteScorer was extracted from CommandRouter so CapabilityBroker can find the best route without importing the router at all, decoupling deterministic routing from the monolithic router class.

hyperedges.label: Turn execution pipeline: ConversationAgent -> CapabilityBroker -> CapabilityExecutor
Context bundle assembly: MemoryBroker aggregates ContextStore + PersonaManager for each turn
Routing decision flow: CommandRouter -> RoutingState -> ResponseFinalizer
Per-turn execution pipeline: TurnManager orchestrates TurnContext, trace_scope, and memory in each turn
ResultCache, OrderedToolExecutor, and TaskGraphExecutor form the tool result caching and execution subsystem
TurnContext, RoutingState, and DialogState form the unified per-turn ephemeral state bundle
v2 Turn Planning Pipeline
Extension Adapter Pattern
Memory Triad (Episodic+Semantic+Procedural)
LangGraph execution pipeline: GraphCompiler + workflows + GraphState
Deterministic routing layer: RouteScorer + ModelRouter + CapabilityBroker
HUD visual state indicators: ParticleGlobeReactor + PulseBars + RadarPanel
Plugin Tool Registration Pattern via app.router
LLM Inference Lock Sharing across research, chat, and file summarization
File Operation Pipeline: search, read, summarize
Voice I/O pipeline: audio capture, transcription, safety gating, command dispatch
Reminder/calendar creation flow: parse datetime, insert event, schedule timer and notification
Double-clap startup pipeline: detect clap, check FRIDAY state, launch FRIDAY, autostart registration
Camera-based perception pipeline: capture, detect, stream
v2 planning layer: intent classification, plan building, workflow resumption
WhatsApp send pipeline: skill -> client -> driver
STT/TTS Voice Pipeline with Barge-In
Router Tool Dispatch and Multi-Action Execution Flow
v2 Turn Orchestration through RuntimeKernel
TurnOrchestrator pipeline: WorkflowCoordinator, IntentEngine, ToolPlan executor
Hybrid architecture app core: CapabilityRegistry, ContextStore, ConversationAgent, WorkflowOrchestrator
Workflow orchestration via plugins: TaskManagerPlugin, SystemControlPlugin, BrowserAutomationPlugin with CommandRouter
